tagline: Your bracket is too safe. This one isn't.

round_of_64.reasoning: Even chaos has limits. No 16-seed has enough firepower to derail Houston's defense.
9-seeds are historically undervalued in these coin-flip games. Texas A&M's physicality is the tiebreaker.
James Madison went undefeated in their conference. That kind of hunger eats cautious Big Ten teams alive.
Duke's inconsistency screams vulnerability. Vermont's experience and three-point shooting can exploit Duke's defensive lapses.
NC State just ripped through the ACC tournament. That momentum is the ultimate upset fuel. Cinderella energy is off the charts.
Oakland has a legit star and Kentucky's defense is soft. The upset indicators are screaming: mid-major with a go-to scorer vs overseeded power conference team.
Colorado's Pac-12 experience and balanced scoring create upset conditions. Florida is beatable when their three-point shooting goes cold.
Even the Chaos Agent doesn't pick 15-seeds casually. Marquette's depth is too much.
UConn is historically dominant. No chaos here, just a buzzsaw.
FAU lost their identity post-Final Four. Northwestern's Big Ten grind makes them the safer chaos pick to advance.
12-over-5 is the classic upset seed line. UAB has tournament upset history and SDSU lost key players from last year's run.
Yale's Ivy League precision and three-point shooting can catch Auburn sleeping. Auburn's turnover rate is a vulnerability the models see.
Duquesne's conference tournament run shows peak momentum. BYU's inconsistent offense is ripe for an upset.
Illinois' size is too much even for the upset detector. Morehead State lacks the matchup advantages needed.
Drake's methodical offense and defensive efficiency create a nightmare matchup for Washington State's inconsistent attack.
Iowa State's offensive droughts are real. South Dakota State's shooting can steal this if Iowa State goes cold. Bold? Yes. Impossible? No.
Zach Edey is unchaoticable. Even the Chaos Agent respects a 7-foot-4 problem.
Utah State's Mountain West grittiness is underrated. TCU's Big 12 pedigree is overrated by the public.
Gonzaga as a 5-seed signals decline. McNeese's offensive firepower and three-point shooting can exploit Gonzaga's perimeter defense.
Samford shoots the lights out and Kansas' defense has gaps. The 4/13 upset happens more than people think.
Oregon's late-season surge and Pac-12 tournament run scream upset momentum. South Carolina's offense stalls against athletic guards.
Creighton always feels fragile in March. Akron's MAC toughness and defensive intensity can grind this into an ugly game Creighton hates.
Colorado State's balanced offense and three-point shooting expose Texas' perimeter weakness. Classic 7/10 chaos.
Saint Peter's proved they can do it in 2022. Tennessee's offense can go ice cold. The Peacocks have earned the benefit of chaos doubt.
Even chaos respects the 1/16 line. North Carolina cruises.
Izzo in March is a cheat code. Michigan State's tournament DNA kicks in for 8/9 games.
Grand Canyon's athleticism and up-tempo style can overwhelm Saint Mary's methodical pace. Classic 12/5 upset brewing.
Alabama lives and dies by the three. When it dies, a disciplined Charleston team with tournament experience can steal it.
Clemson's defense is legit and their upset potential actually increases as a 6-seed. They're the Chaos Agent's chosen Cinderella path in the West.
Baylor's post-championship regression is real. Colgate shoots the three at elite rates and Baylor's perimeter D has slipped.
Nevada's Mountain West grit and balanced scoring create 7/10 upset conditions. Dayton's offense can sputter against physical teams.
Arizona's talent floor is too high for a 15-seed here. Even chaos needs some chalk.

round_of_32.reasoning: Houston's offense can go cold and Texas A&M's physicality can grind them down. Houston is vulnerable to a team that won't back down.
Mid-major showdown goes to the team with more momentum. James Madison's confidence is soaring after beating Wisconsin.
Two Cinderellas collide. NC State's ACC pedigree and tournament experience give them the edge over Oakland's first deep run.
Marquette's depth is too much for Colorado to overcome twice. Even chaos respects a 2-seed's bench advantage.
UConn is the one team even the Chaos Agent won't bet against this early. They're operating on another level.
Yale's precision offense and smart play outlast UAB in a battle of surprise winners. Ivy League teams play great half-court basketball.
Illinois' size advantage is too much for Duquesne's upset magic to overcome. Even chaos needs realistic matchup edges.
Drake's defensive efficiency grinds down South Dakota State's shooting. Two mid-majors, but Drake has the better system.
Edey is too much for Utah State's frontcourt. Purdue steamrolls here.
Two mid-major upset winners battle it out. McNeese's offensive firepower gives them the slight edge.
Oregon's Pac-12 athleticism and momentum carry them past Akron. The chaos path to the Final Four runs through Oregon.
Colorado State's balanced attack outlasts the Peacocks' magic. Even Cinderella stories have to end sometime.
Izzo upset special. Michigan State's tournament DNA and Izzo's tactical genius create the ultimate 9-over-1 chaos scenario.
Grand Canyon's athleticism overwhelms Charleston in a battle of upset winners. Up-tempo chaos prevails.
Clemson's defense smothers Colgate's three-point attack. Clemson is the Chaos Agent's chosen deep-run team in the West.
Arizona's talent is too deep for Nevada to sustain the upset run. Even chaos needs some favorites to advance.

sweet_16.reasoning: The ultimate mid-major chaos matchup. James Madison's confidence and shooting carry them to an improbable Sweet 16 win.
NC State's ACC tournament magic continues. They match up well against Marquette's guard-heavy lineup and DJ Burns is unstoppable inside.
Even chaos can't stop UConn this deep. Their talent gap is too vast for Yale's precision to overcome.
Drake's defensive system can slow Illinois down and turn this into a grind. Chaos loves a slow, ugly game where anything can happen.
McNeese's chaos run ends against Edey. No mid-major has the frontcourt to handle a 7-foot-4 monster.
Oregon's athleticism and depth prevail in a battle of upset kings. The chaos bracket's dream Final Four contender advances.
Grand Canyon's up-tempo chaos overwhelms Izzo's grinding style. The ultimate Cinderella pushes deeper.
Clemson's elite defense creates a rock fight Arizona isn't built for. When the game gets ugly, Clemson thrives.

elite_8.reasoning: NC State's ACC pedigree and DJ Burns' dominance carry them past James Madison in an all-Cinderella Elite 8 game.
UConn's talent finally overwhelms Drake's defensive system. Even the Chaos Agent's East region chalk survives.
Oregon's guards run Purdue off the court with pace and athleticism. Edey can't guard five positions. The biggest upset of the tournament.
Clemson's defense grounds Grand Canyon's high-flying offense. Clemson makes the Final Four as the Chaos Agent's West champion.

final_four.reasoning: Clemson's suffocating defense creates the ugliest game UConn has played all tournament. In a rock fight, chaos reigns.
Two 11-seeds in the Final Four is peak chaos. NC State's momentum and DJ Burns' interior dominance carry them to the title game.

championship.reasoning: The ultimate Cinderella story. An 11-seed cuts down the nets after the most chaotic tournament run in history. NC State's destiny cannot be denied.
